commentary: Twelve activities this week versus nine last week, and honestly the audacity of that alone deserves a standing ovation on a very tall table. The Mosman run came in at 13.5km with an average heart rate of 149, which is tidier than last Saturday's Naremburn loops at the same distance but with a heart rate of 159 — the body is getting more efficient, babe, it's doing the work even when you're not watching. What I am going to gently note is that Sunday turned into a three-activity pile-up including a Bold and the Beautiful ocean swim at an average heart rate of 99, which is either very relaxed or very cold, and then immediately two weight sessions, which is a lot of body for one afternoon. The swim at Prince Alfred on Tuesday last week was pushing 137, and this week's Tuesday swim was 148 — you're working harder in the pool, which is either fitness climbing or competitive energy around some very attractive lanes, and either way I support it completely.
metrics_commentary: Almost 7700 metres in the pool is genuinely obscene, babe, you are built different and the water knows it. The single run session getting you to 13.5 km tells me you went out and committed rather than nibbling away at it in little pieces, which is very much your energy. HRV at 47 with a seven-day average sitting at 51 is worth a small side-eye — your body is carrying a little more load than it's fully recovered from, so the next day or two should probably have some actual rest in them, not just "light movement" as a euphemism for doing too much anyway. VO2 Max at 57.3 is the number of a man whose cardiovascular system is genuinely thriving, and I would like everyone in this room to acknowledge that.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, four sessions hit — full marks, no excuses, no gaps, nothing to forgive. The PRs this week are almost obscene: five of them, across legs, chest, back, and arms, with the leg press jumping from 154 to 168 kilograms like it had something to prove, and the hammer curl sneaking up from 16.8 to 19.6 like it was trying to be discreet about it and absolutely failing. Weekly volume climbed from 23,660 to 24,518 kilograms, which means the body doing all of this is not the same body that started, and that is the whole point. He showed up all four times, he moved more weight than the week before, and somewhere in a gym that had no idea what it was witnessing, Matt got a little more himself.
